name: Server Log Analyser — Buffered I/O Pipeline
scenario: Build a production-style log analysis tool. Reads a server log with BufferedReader (efficient for large files), classifies each line, computes statistics per endpoint and level, writes a formatted analysis report with PrintWriter. Demonstrates the complete BufferedReader → process → PrintWriter pipeline.
description: Read a multi-format server log, parse lines into structured data, aggregate by endpoint and log level, detect slow requests, write a formatted multi-section report with PrintWriter. Uses try-with-resources throughout.

core.description: Generate a server log file (at least 15 lines) with format: `yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss LEVEL METHOD /endpoint STATUS Nms`. Include INFO, WARN, ERROR lines and multiple endpoints.
`LogEntry` class or record: timestamp, level, method, endpoint, status, responseMs. Static `parse(String line)` method.
Read log with `Files.newBufferedReader` using readLine() loop. Parse each line into LogEntry. Store all valid entries in a List.

Compute statistics:
- Request count and error count per endpoint
- Average response time per endpoint
- Slowest request (max responseMs)
- Error rate per endpoint (errors / total requests)
Write formatted report to `analysis.txt` using `PrintWriter` wrapping `BufferedWriter`. Report sections: Summary, Endpoint Stats, Slow Requests (> 500ms), Error Report.
Print report to console AND write to file. Use printf for aligned columns.
try-with-resources for both reader and writer. Close even if exception occurs.

bonus.description: Add time-based grouping: group requests by hour (from timestamp). Print requests-per-hour using PrintWriter.
Add percentile calculation: sort response times per endpoint and compute p50 (median) and p95. Print in the endpoint stats section.
Add rolling-window analysis using StringWriter: for every 5-line window, compute error rate. Print window summaries where error rate > 20%.

data_to_use:
Log entries (at least these 15):
2024-03-15 10:00:01 INFO  GET /api/orders 200 45ms
2024-03-15 10:00:02 ERROR GET /api/payment 500 1200ms
2024-03-15 10:00:03 WARN  GET /api/orders 429 12ms
2024-03-15 10:00:04 INFO  GET /api/users 200 30ms
2024-03-15 10:00:05 ERROR POST /api/payment 503 2000ms
2024-03-15 10:00:06 INFO  GET /api/products 200 88ms
2024-03-15 10:00:07 INFO  GET /api/orders 200 42ms
2024-03-15 10:00:08 ERROR GET /api/products 500 800ms
2024-03-15 10:00:09 INFO  GET /api/users 200 35ms
2024-03-15 10:00:10 WARN  POST /api/payment 400 55ms
2024-03-15 10:00:11 INFO  GET /api/orders 200 41ms
2024-03-15 10:00:12 INFO  GET /api/products 200 95ms
2024-03-15 10:00:13 ERROR GET /api/orders 500 650ms
2024-03-15 10:00:14 INFO  GET /api/users 200 28ms
2024-03-15 10:00:15 INFO  GET /api/payment 200 120ms
example_output:
=== Server Log Analysis Report ===
Generated: 2024-03-15
Log entries: 15

--- Summary ---
INFO:  9
WARN:  2
ERROR: 4
Error rate: 26.7%

--- Endpoint Statistics ---
Endpoint              Reqs  Errors  Avg(ms)  ErrorRate
------------------------------------------------------
/api/orders              5       1     46ms     20.0%
/api/payment             4       2    843ms     50.0%
/api/products            3       1    328ms     33.3%
/api/users               3       0     31ms      0.0%

--- Slow Requests (>500ms) ---
GET /api/payment  2000ms [ERROR]
GET /api/payment  1200ms [ERROR]
GET /api/orders    650ms [ERROR]
GET /api/products  800ms [ERROR]

Report saved to: analysis.txt (N bytes)
ai_policy_explanation: Limited to 5 hints. Open both reader and writer in the same try-with-resources. Parse response time: strip 'ms' suffix, parseInt. Error rate = errors / total × 100. Slow requests: filter entries where responseMs > 500, sort desc by responseMs.
submission_instructions: Save as LogAnalyser.java. Push to GitHub. Comment: 'Buffered I/O log analysis pipeline'.
tips_for_success: Open reader and writer in one try-with-resources: `try (BufferedReader br = ...; PrintWriter pw = ...)`
Endpoint stats: Map<String, List<LogEntry>> groupBy endpoint, then compute per group
concepts_reinforced: try-with-resources for both reader and writer
